last_item: Band D (Query): Query → shadcn. Rebuilt src/views/Query.tsx. SQL <textarea class='sql-editor'> → shadcn Textarea (added via `shadcn add textarea`); editor + actions row now flex/gap-* instead of .editor-wrap/.editor-actions chrome. Sample .chip buttons → Button variant='outline' size='sm' (+ title disclosing they replace the editor). Run → Button composing the raw Spinner (data-icon='inline-start') + 'Running…' busy; dropped the redundant standalone results Spinner so busy state matches the app-wide inline pattern. Results .data-table → Table family; .row-num/.cell-null/.col-name/.col-type markup → Tailwind tokens (tabular-nums, text-muted-foreground, italic). A11y: editor aria-label 'Read-only SQL editor' → 'SQL query editor' (field is editable; read-only is carried by ReadOnlyBadge + Callout). Behavior identical: Cmd/Ctrl+Enter to run, read-first guard, auto-LIMIT badge+note, zero-row/zero-column messaging, ErrorNotice. Deleted dead Query-only CSS (.chip/.query-*/.editor-*/.sql-editor/.kbd-hint/.results*); kept shared .data-table/.table-scroll/.row-num etc (Alerts/Backups/Migrate still use them). Added Query.test.tsx (5 tests).
last_result: green: typecheck, 111 web tests (+5), build, go build (outside sandbox; snap-confine cap_dac_override blocks go in-sandbox). ui-heuristics-reviewer: accepted 3 findings (#1 double spinner → removed standalone results Spinner; #2 sample buttons silently replace editor → added title tooltip disclosure; #3 'Read-only SQL editor' aria-label misleading on an editable field → 'SQL query editor'); declined #4 (replace zero-row table with a Callout) — the column headers convey result shape at zero rows and that matches prior behavior. Next in band D: Backups. Still deferred to band E: --info-soft collides with --primary-soft; prune next-themes dep.
notes: PURE UI/UX LOOP. The prior 57-iteration hardening track is COMPLETE and shipped as v0.6.0. This track rebuilds the web panel on shadcn/ui — see PROMPT.md + UI-RULES.md + backlog.md. The operator EXPLICITLY authorized the shadcn/Tailwind/Radix dependency tree, overriding the earlier rule-4/5 objection — do NOT re-litigate whether to adopt shadcn; it is required. Start at band A (scaffold), then B (shell), C (primitives), D (views one at a time), E (cleanup). Never touch Go/business logic — frontend only.
north_star: Every view in the indiepg panel rebuilt with shadcn/ui — clean, simple, consistent — with no hand-rolled component where a shadcn one exists, behavior identical, all tests green.
